entities.name: Master Peter of Ardene
entities.raw_quote: a certain surgeon from my home country, and in fact even a native of my very parish, named Master Peter of Ardene

entities.name: Spanish magician
entities.raw_quote: a certain Spanish magician [magus] was on close terms with him

entities.name: demon summoned by the Spanish magician

attributes.behavioral_trait: obeys ritual timing tied to liturgical calendar

entities.raw_quote: the demon...delayed his coming...mourning and sighing...‘You people are really strange [Mirum est de vobis], for the angels in heaven are celebrating the feast of the Virgin Mary, and you cannot take your rest here on earth!’

entities.name: conjure / adjure / exorcize
entities.raw_quote: the terms ‘conjuration’ and ‘exorcism’ are essentially interchangeable in medieval usage

entities.name: Rator, Lampoy, Despan, Brulo, Dronoth, Maloqui, Satola, Gelbid, Mascifm, Nartim, Lodoni

attributes.role: named demonic names invoked in conjuration

entities.raw_quote: by these names, which cause you fear and terror: Rator, Lampoy, Despan, Brulo, Dronoth, Maloqui, Satola, Gelbid, Mascifm, Nartim and Lodoni

entities.name: Vsyr, Salaul, Silitor, Demor, Zanno, Syrtroy, Risbel, Cutroy, Lytay, Onor, Moloy, Pumotor, Tami, Oor, Ym

attributes.function: bear arms and deceive human senses

entities.raw_quote: O Vsyr, Salaul, Silitor, Demor, Zanno, Syrtroy, Risbel, Cutroy, Lytay, Onor, Moloy, Pumotor, Tami, Oor and Ym, arms-bearing spirits, whose role it is to bear arms and to deceive human senses wherever you wish

entities.name: Aa, Ely, Sother, Adonay, Cel, Sabaoth, Messyas, Alazabra, Osian

attributes.effect: arouse fear in all things in heaven, earth, and hell

entities.raw_quote: by these most precious and ineffable names of the Creator of all...to wit Aa, Ely, Sother, Adonay, Cel, Sabaoth, Messyas, Alazabra and Osian

entities.name: circle
entities.raw_quote: made a circle according to his art

entities.name: pseudo-Chaldean incantation
entities.raw_quote: One notable exception is a conjuration given in pseudo-Chaldean (no. 18): Bismille araathe....

entities.name: Rawlinson manuscript
entities.raw_quote: In the Rawlinson manuscript, the text shifts to the vernacular particularly for formulas addressed to the spirits when they have come

relationships.to: demon summoned by the Spanish magician

summary: This chunk focuses on the structure and function of conjurations in the Munich Handbook (CLM 849), analyzing their formal elements: declaration, address, invocations, and instruction. It includes detailed examples from nos. 3 and 7, listing named spirits (e.g., Rator et al.; Vsyr et al.) and divine/demonic names used for coercion. A narrative exemplum involving Master Peter of Ardene and a Spanish magician illustrates ritual summoning and demon responses tied to liturgical time. The text also notes linguistic features (Latin vs. vernacular), cross-cultural comparisons with Jewish magic, and references to other manuscripts like the Rawlinson and pseudo-Chaldean texts.
key_passages: The conjurations are so centrally important to necromantic experiments that the art of necromancy...can even be referred to simply as the conjuring of spirits.
[Declaration:] I conjure [Address:] you demons inscribed in this circle, to whom is given the power of seducing and binding women in the love of men...

O Vsyr, Salaul, Silitor, Demor, Zanno, Syrtroy, Risbel, Cutroy, Lytay, Onor, Moloy, Pumotor, Tami, Oor and Ym, arms-bearing spirits, whose role it is to bear arms and to deceive human senses wherever you wish...
by these names, which cause you fear and terror: Rator, Lampoy, Despan, Brulo, Dronoth, Maloqui, Satola, Gelbid, Mascifm, Nartim and Lodoni...
the terms ‘conjuration’ and ‘exorcism’ are essentially interchangeable in medieval usage
